objective: Full runtime binding proof for target object DIR (passport: governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json) within the complete governance object chain GD -> DIR -> WRK. Prove Claude as the active provider for DIR object runtime binding, establish auditor-validated executor dispatch through WRK-C1 as the downstream contour, and confirm idempotent Telegram routing from GD through DIR to WRK with trace-based deduplication.
assumptions: Target object DIR is the mid-chain governance object (canonical_name: Директор, passport: governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json) with parent_object_id=GD and child_object_ids=[WRK]. The full object chain is GD -> DIR -> WRK per objects_registry_v2.json (registry_id=objects_registry_v2, schema_version=1.0).

DIR carries two registered contours: DIR-C1 (canonical_name: Контур правил директора, managed_channel: governance/channels/DIR-C1.jsonl) and DIR-C2 (canonical_name: Контур решений директора, managed_channel: governance/channels/DIR-C2.jsonl). Both contain roles ANALYST, AUDITOR, EXECUTOR. DIR object runtime binding must address both contours.

Claude provider is active for executor and auditor roles per governance/state/routing.json v6 (auditor.active=claude, executor.active=claude). claude_code status=ok in provider_status.json v3. The GD probe trace bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_gd_a1 completed with outcome=success, confirming the upstream chain head is already bound. This DIR probe is the second sequential step in bem934_objects_bound_runner dispatch sequence.

DIR passport currently declares runtime_provider=codex_local and status=PENDING_RUNTIME_BINDING. The object runtime binding probe will transition this entry to provider=claude and status=BOUND as part of the full chain binding via claude.yml workflow dispatches orchestrated by bem934_objects_bound_runner.py.

WRK-C1 (contour_id=WRK-C1, managed_channel=governance/channels/WRK-C1.jsonl, canonical_name: Первый внутренний контур работника) is the canonical downstream executor contour for the entire GD -> DIR -> WRK chain. DIR runtime binding must verify that executor dispatch from DIR reaches WRK-C1.EXECUTOR via the channel layer, making WRK-C1 the proof point for DIR downstream routing.

Idempotent Telegram routing: delivery of trace_id bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a1 through the Telegram/channel layer targeting governance/channels/WRK-C1.jsonl produces exactly one executor dispatch entry. Deduplication is enforced by trace_id uniqueness checks in managed_channel_consumer.py before writing to executor channel.

The materializer governance/runners/bem934_object_binding_materializer.py performs two validation layers for DIR: semantic_checks (required fields, trace_id match, keyword presence including dir, wrk-c1, claude, provider, object, runtime, binding, idempotent, telegram, routing) and exercise_auditor_pre (isolated tempdir auditor stage runner verifying WRK-C1 channel write and executor routing). DIR contour channels DIR-C1.jsonl and DIR-C2.jsonl must also be verified writable.

DIR curator runner is registered at governance/runners/dir_curator_runner.py. This runner owns DIR.CURATOR role and must be verifiable as syntactically valid and importable before binding is finalised. The curator_id DIR.CURATOR is the rule_owner for DIR governance objects.
steps: Step 1 — Provider health pre-check for DIR probe: Before dispatching claude.yml for DIR, confirm claude_code.status=ok in governance/state/provider_status.json and claude.last_outcome=success from the immediately preceding GD probe (trace bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_gd_a1). Confirm routing.json executor.active=claude and auditor.active=claude. If any check fails apply fallback_chain from role_sequence.json (on_provider_limit: switch_provider_keep_role).

Step 2 — DIR binding probe dispatch: Dispatch claude.yml with inputs role=analyst, provider=claude, task_type=object_runtime_binding, trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a1. The task prompt must explicitly name target object DIR, passport path governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json, parent object GD (passport governance/architecture/object_passports/GD.json), full chain GD -> DIR -> WRK, Claude as provider, both DIR contours DIR-C1 and DIR-C2, WRK-C1 as downstream executor contour, and idempotent Telegram routing. Poll SOURCE_RECEIPT governance/proofs/BEM934_claude_result_materialization_receipt.json every 15s up to 16 polls for trace_id match and status=PASS.

Step 3 — DIR semantic validation via materializer: Run bem934_object_binding_materializer.py with TRACE_ID=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a1, CLAUDE_OUTCOME=success. Confirm all semantic_checks pass: strict_json_object_parsed, required_fields_present (objective/assumptions/steps/acceptance/risks/trace_id), trace_matches, steps_are_task_specific (>=3 non-empty), acceptance_is_verifiable (>=2 non-empty), mentions_dir_target_object, mentions_wrk_c1, mentions_claude_provider, mentions_object_runtime_binding, mentions_idempotent_telegram_routing, historical_fixed_plan_absent.

Step 4 — DIR auditor pre-check via exercise_auditor_pre: Materializer invokes auditor_stage_runner in isolated tempdir targeting DIR. Confirm checks: real_pre_channel_consumed=True (runner_result=WRK-C1_pre_check_pass via DIR routing path), executor_channel_written=True (wrk_c1_to_executor.jsonl written with dir_source=True), executor_route_selected=True (to=WRK-C1.EXECUTOR, routed_through=DIR), semantic_pre_check_pass=True (pre_check=PASS), trace_preserved=True (trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a1), result_artifact_emitted=True. Verify DIR-C1.jsonl and DIR-C2.jsonl are both writable in the isolated tempdir.

Step 5 — DIR curator runner validation: Confirm governance/runners/dir_curator_runner.py is syntactically valid and importable (python -c import check, no ImportError, no SyntaxError). Confirm DIR.CURATOR identity is correctly declared as rule_owner in the runner. Record runner_check=PASS in the DIR binding evidence block.

Step 6 — DIR registry entry update: objects_bound_runner writes DIR binding evidence to governance/proofs/bem934_objects_bound/DIR_runtime_binding.json containing: object_id=DIR, passport_path=governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json, trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a1, provider=claude, workflow=.github/workflows/claude.yml, contours_bound=[DIR-C1, DIR-C2], downstream_contour=WRK-C1, source_receipt_blob_sha, source_plan_blob_sha, source_commit_sha. Updates DIR entry in objects_registry_v2.json with runtime_binding block: status=BOUND, bound_at=<utc_now>.

Step 7 — Idempotent Telegram routing verification for DIR trace: Replay trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a1 twice through the Telegram routing layer targeting WRK-C1.jsonl. Confirm managed_channel_consumer.py deduplicates: second delivery is no-op, only one executor entry written bearing this trace_id. Verify by line-count on governance/channels/WRK-C1.jsonl before and after replay — count must not increase on second delivery.

Step 8 — Chain continuity confirmation GD -> DIR -> WRK: Confirm chain state: GD passport status=BOUND (prior probe), DIR passport status=BOUND (this probe), WRK passport status=PENDING_RUNTIME_BINDING (WRK probe is next). objects_registry_v2.json must show GD and DIR with runtime_binding blocks and WRK remaining PENDING. Append DIR completed record to governance/transport/results.jsonl with trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a1, status=completed, provider=claude, blocker=null.
acceptance: governance/proofs/bem934_objects_bound/DIR_runtime_binding.json is present and contains: object_id=DIR, passport_path=governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json, trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a1, provider=claude, workflow=.github/workflows/claude.yml, contours_bound=[DIR-C1, DIR-C2], downstream_contour=WRK-C1, runner_check=PASS, source_receipt_blob_sha non-empty, source_commit_sha non-empty.

objects_registry_v2.json DIR entry carries runtime_binding block with status=BOUND, provider=claude, trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a1, and explicitly references passport_path=governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json. GD entry is already BOUND from prior probe. WRK entry remains PENDING_RUNTIME_BINDING awaiting WRK probe.

DIR materializer receipt has status=PASS with all semantic_checks=True, including mentions_dir_target_object, mentions_wrk_c1, mentions_claude_provider, mentions_object_runtime_binding, mentions_idempotent_telegram_routing; exercise_auditor_pre checks all True: real_pre_channel_consumed, executor_route_selected=WRK-C1.EXECUTOR, trace_preserved for trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a1.

governance/channels/WRK-C1.jsonl contains exactly one entry for trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a1 after two replay deliveries; second delivery produced no additional line (idempotency confirmed for DIR trace).
governance/transport/results.jsonl contains entry with trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a1, role=analyst, provider=claude, status=completed, blocker=null, non-empty commit_sha.
risks: Upstream dependency on GD probe: DIR probe dispatches only after GD probe status=BOUND. If GD binding commit is missing or registry write conflicted, DIR probe will encounter inconsistent chain state. Mitigation: Step 1 pre-check verifies claude.last_outcome=success for GD trace before dispatching DIR.

DIR runtime_provider declared as codex_local in passport — stale value not reflecting the intended Claude binding. Materializer semantic_checks must NOT reject the plan for this discrepancy; the probe purpose is to bind DIR to Claude. Mitigation: materializer targets mentions_claude_provider keyword in the generated plan, not in the passport field, so passport staleness does not block validation.

DIR-C1 and DIR-C2 channel writability: if governance/channels/DIR-C1.jsonl or DIR-C2.jsonl do not exist on disk, exercise_auditor_pre will fail channel-write checks. Mitigation: Step 4 explicitly requires both channel files to be writable in tempdir; runner creates them if absent within the isolation boundary.

Idempotency collision between GD and DIR traces: if managed_channel_consumer.py de-duplicates at trace_id level only (not trace_id + object_id), replaying DIR trace through the same WRK-C1.jsonl channel could cause cross-trace false-positive deduplication. Mitigation: Step 7 verifies a new distinct entry IS written on first DIR delivery, confirming per-trace deduplication is object-trace-scoped.

Registry write conflict under concurrent DIR/WRK probes: if bem934_objects_bound_runner dispatches DIR and WRK in overlapping windows, objects_registry_v2.json could be corrupted. Mitigation: runner enforces sequential dispatch — WRK probe not queued until Step 8 chain continuity check confirms DIR entry is committed and registry_blob_sha is recorded.
